title: cymbopogon proximus var. sennarensis
intro: Cymbopogon proximus var. sennarensis, also known as East Indian lemongrass, is a species of grass native to the Ethiopian highlands. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Cymbopogon proximus var. sennarensis include relief from digestive issues, sore throats, and skin irritations, as well as its potential to reduce stress and promote relaxation. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, essential oil, or infusions, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
